summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that a deeply nested XML document reaches the default post-parse normalization path and crashes the Node process with a call-stack exhaustion RangeError.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should feed a deeply nested XML document into DOMParser.parseFromString and trigger RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded from repeated Element.normalize frames on the default parser path.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned commit 9fb2667c25ea4e9c290498e0d911904b1ef6f336, built the reproduction image, mounted the cloned checkout, and printed '[evohunt] parsing nested XML depth=10000 bytes=70000' followed by 'RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded' with repeated '/target-repo/lib/dom.js' Element.normalize frames. The verifier then printed '[evohunt] verification confirmed stack exhaustion on the default parse path' and run.sh exited successfully.
verification_method: The verifier runs the exploit without catching the exception and requires a non-zero Node failure plus the exact RangeError message and Element.normalize stack frames. This distinguishes the vulnerability from an unrelated startup failure or mount problem.
evidence_produced_by_run: The cloned target at commit 9fb2667c25ea4e9c290498e0d911904b1ef6f336 threw RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded while parsing a 10,000-level nested XML document.
The stack trace contained repeated Element.normalize frames under /target-repo/lib/dom.js, tying the failure to the recursive normalization path.
The verifier printed the expected success marker after matching the crash signature.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container.
